title: Rustic Wall Clip Frame
summary: An open 1x2 frame with wood clothespins glued across the middle — clip in photos, cards, or artwork and swap them out anytime, no glass required.
description:
This is a picture frame with no picture in it — instead of glass over a fixed photo, clothespins glued across the front let you clip in and swap out whatever you want displayed, whenever you want. That makes it a genuinely useful piece rather than a one-time decoration.

The frame itself is simple butt-jointed 1x2s, so the only thing to watch is square: check the diagonal measurements from opposite corners after assembly and adjust before the glue sets, because a slightly out-of-square frame is very noticeable on a plain open rectangle with nothing else to distract the eye.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four frame ends at 23-3/4" and two side rails at 26-1/2" from the Pine 1x2. Cut one center bar at 23-3/4" from the Pine 1x3. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft Pine 1x2 and one 2 ft Pine 1x3. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Assemble the outer frame
steps.body: The two 26-1/2" side rails run the full height of the frame and every 23-3/4" cross member fits between them, so start by joining one frame end across the top and one across the bottom to make the outer rectangle. Lay the 1x2s face-forward — wide 1-1/2" face toward you — because that face is what the clothespins glue to later, and it is what makes the finished frame 26-3/4" wide by 26-1/2" tall. Glue every butt joint and clamp it, then pin each corner by driving 1-1/4" brads at an angle through the rail's 3/4"-thick back face into the end of the cross member; driven straight in from the outside edge a 1-1/4" brad would have to cross the rail's full 1-1/2" width and would never reach. Measure both diagonals from opposite outside corners and adjust until they match before the glue sets — on a plain open rectangle an out-of-square corner is the only thing there is to look at.

steps.title: Add the interior cross members
steps.body: Three cross members remain: the two leftover 23-3/4" 1x2 frame ends and the 23-3/4" 1x3 center bar. Space them across the 26-1/2" opening — the 1x3 center bar in the middle, where its 2-1/2" face gives the widest landing for a clothespin, and a 1x2 end between it and each of the top and bottom rails. Mark both side rails together from the same end so each cross member lands square, then glue and toenail two 1-1/4" brads per joint through the back of the rail, the same way you fastened the corners.

steps.title: Stain and add the clothespins
steps.body: Stain the whole frame and let it dry fully before any glue touches it — staining around glued-on clothespins is miserable and the glue squeeze-out will resist the stain. Glue the clothespins to the front faces of the cross members with the jaws pointing up, one on the centerline of each member and one 6-1/2" out to each side; the twelve clothespins cover four members at three apiece. Press each one down until glue squeezes out and leave the frame flat until it cures, so nothing slides while it sets.

steps.title: Hang it
steps.body: Attach a picture-hanging kit to the back and hang.

images.alt: A rustic wood wall frame with clothespins glued across the front for clipping in photos
